Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania
prices.

📍 Philadelphia, PA Penn Medicine 5/5 CMS rating

Cash-pay and insurance prices for 29 procedures at Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania, pulled from the federally-mandated machine-readable file the hospital is required to publish. Cash-pay range across these procedures: $20 to $19,502.

Procedure prices.

  • Abdominal ultrasound, complete CPT 76700 $1,819
  • Brain MRI with and without contrast CPT 70553 $19,502
  • Brain MRI without contrast CPT 70551 $9,957
  • Carotid duplex ultrasound CPT 93880 $564 to $5,407
  • CBC with differential CPT 85025 $273
  • Chest X-ray, single view CPT 71045 $374 to $471
  • Colonoscopy with polyp removal CPT 45385 $6,195
  • Colonoscopy, diagnostic CPT 45378 $418 to $4,927
  • Comprehensive metabolic panel CPT 80053 $368
  • CT abdomen and pelvis with contrast CPT 74177 $7,194
  • DXA bone density scan CPT 77080 $2,171
  • Echocardiogram, complete with Doppler CPT 93306 $499 to $10,616
  • Hemoglobin A1c CPT 83036 $42 to $174
  • Inguinal hernia repair, open CPT 49505 $13,591
  • Knee/lower-extremity MRI without contrast CPT 73721 $9,675 to $9,744
  • Lipid panel CPT 80061 $480
  • Lumbar epidural steroid injection CPT 64483 $822 to $2,366

  • Office visit, established patient, level 4 CPT 99214 $518

CMS quality rating.

5/5 stars on CMS Hospital Care Compare. Based on ~50 measures of safety, mortality, readmission, patient experience, and timeliness. View on Care Compare ↗

Patient experience.

CMS HCAHPS patient survey, period ending 03/31/2025 · 605 completed surveys.

Measure This hospital Network avg Δ
HCAHPS overall star rating 4/5 2.8/5 +1.2
Would definitely recommend 84% 69.6% +14pp
Hospital rating 9 or 10 of 10 84% 68.6% +15pp
Nurses always communicated well 86% 75.6% +10pp
Doctors always communicated well 82% 76.6% +5pp
Given clear info about recovery 90% 84.4% +6pp
Room and bathroom always clean 76% 68.6% +7pp
Staff always explained meds 66% 57.6% +8pp
Quiet at night, always 64% 52.2% +12pp

About this data.

The prices above are pulled directly from Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania's machine-readable file (MRF), required under the Hospital Price Transparency Rule (45 CFR 180.50). We download the file, parse the rows for our shoppable CPT set, and publish the dollar amounts as the hospital published them. No surveys, no estimates.

Cash-pay rates are what an uninsured patient would be charged. Insurance-negotiated rates vary by payer and plan; pick a payer in the comparison tool to see plan-specific numbers.

Data sources: CMS Hospital Price Transparency rule (45 CFR 180.50). CMS Hospital Care Compare (xubh-q36u). Last refresh: 2026-04-27.
A consumer reading of CMS-mandated MRF data. Not medical or financial advice. Itemized · itemized.health
